llcFilesWith: Prothonotary of the Superior Court in each Delaware county (New Castle, Kent, or Sussex) where the business is transacted (Registration of Trade, Business & Fictitious Name Certificate)
soleProprietorFilesWith: Prothonotary of the Superior Court in each county where the business is transacted (the same county-court registration applies to individuals and sole proprietors)
feeBasis: The Prothonotary's fee is $25 per county; a business operating in more than one county must file with each county's Prothonotary.
renewalRule: The registration stays in effect until the owner files a termination affidavit; there is no periodic renewal, and there is no fee to terminate.
protectionNote: The trade name certificate discloses who is behind a business name and grants no exclusive right to the name.
searchNote: Delaware trade names are held by the county Prothonotary offices, and the Superior Court runs a trade name search that is separate from the Division of Corporations entity search.
notes: Delaware is unusual: its DBA is not a Secretary of State filing at all. LLCs and sole proprietors register a Trade, Business & Fictitious Name Certificate with the Prothonotary of the Superior Court in each county where they do business (6 Del. C. ch. 31), $25 per county, with no expiration and no fee to terminate. Because it is county-court based, a Delaware trade name search runs through the courts rather than the Division of Corporations.
